message:
feat: add env-override probe roots and refine detection paths for aider, codex, and pi_agent

Introduce two new utility functions and an environment-variable override
mechanism for agent detection probe roots:

- `cwd_join()`: constructs probe paths rooted at the current working
  directory instead of $HOME, enabling detection of per-project markers
  (e.g. `.aider.chat.history.md` in the project root).

- `env_override_roots()`: reads connector-specific environment variables
  (`CASS_AIDER_DATA_ROOT`, `CODEX_HOME`, `PI_CODING_AGENT_DIR`) to allow
  users and CI systems to redirect detection to custom data directories.
  When an override is set, `entry_from_detect()` uses it exclusively and
  tags the source as "env" rather than "default".

Probe path refinements:
- aider: additionally check CWD for `.aider.chat.history.md` so
  project-local aider sessions are detected without a home-dir marker.
- codex: narrow probe from `.codex` + `.config/codex` to the more
  specific `.codex/sessions` sub-path, reducing false positives.
- pi_agent: remove the broad `.pi/agent` root and keep only the
  `.pi/agent/sessions` sub-path for the same reason.
- amp: reformat the long `.config/Code/User/globalStorage/sourcegraph.amp`
  path array for readability (no behavioral change).

Test vector formatting is also condensed from multi-line to single-line
for the expected slug list assertion.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
